report: Codex执政官发布能力闭环考古与稳定开发流程重构报告-2026-07-23.md

access_paths.fact: KnowledgeRadar native MCP tool surface was available and health summary was ok.
Deep research route selected generic web and GitHub repository evidence; no browser login or account action was used.

local_evidence.fact: plugin is the final installable product; repository checkout is not the installation directory; release-impact changes require final stage/zip validation.
Global rules already require same-artifact publication, remote verification and accurate post-merge states.
PR candidate and main publication use the same shared pipeline, including final artifact runtime acceptance and historical mutation checks.
The release zip includes both plugin and source scripts, while installation promotes plugin as the stable product surface.
Final zip acceptance starts bundled MCP, validates runtime identity and probes packaged canary bootstrap, but does not execute a generic public-capability scenario registry.
Bundled MCP smoke verifies tool names, contract identity, provider operations data and evaluation-suite read access.
Known data-loss incidents are guarded by manual mutations for runtime contract, evaluation suite and provider operations data.
The source-only evaluation initializer preserves classified task contracts and can prepare a durable plan.
The stable plugin script directory does not contain initialize-codex-praetor-evaluation.ps1.
MCP exposes codex_praetor_evaluation_suite but no prepare-evaluation equivalent.
Generic plan creation is separate from the evaluation initializer and cannot provide the latter's classified contract by itself.
Five post-feature repairs document provider-data packaging, evaluation-data packaging, task-kind loss, provider scope change and packaged canary bootstrap.
The four capability PRs and five subsequent repairs were merged sequentially on 2026-07-22 and 2026-07-23.

external_evidence.fact: Artifact attestations bind an artifact to build provenance but must be verified and do not themselves prove the artifact is secure or functionally correct.
external_evidence.use_in_report: Separate artifact identity assurance from capability completion assurance.

external_evidence.fact: Immutable releases lock release assets and tags; GitHub recommends attaching all assets to a draft before publication.
external_evidence.use_in_report: Catch internal product gaps before merge/publication instead of replacing release assets later.

external_evidence.fact: Provenance should accompany the artifact and be bound to it; provenance and artifact verification are separate from policy/content evaluation.
external_evidence.use_in_report: Retain same-artifact release evidence while adding functional scenario evidence.

external_evidence.fact: The upstream Inspector describes itself as a visual testing tool for MCP servers; its README documents running it against a built server command.
external_evidence.use_in_report: Support protocol-level testing of the bundled MCP rather than relying only on source functions.

external_evidence.fact: Contract tests capture a shared understanding of consumer/provider behavior instead of relying only on broad integration tests.
external_evidence.use_in_report: Model public capability scenarios as executable contracts.

known_limits: No source can prove Qoder, CodeBuddy, Codex Desktop, GitHub or the network will never change externally.
This report identifies a verified evaluation-preparation gap and structural test gaps; it does not claim that every future product bug has already been enumerated.
No real provider dispatch was performed in this read-only report task.
